questionHtml:
Calculate the amount, in mol, of sulfur dioxide produced when 500.0 g of lignite undergoes combustion.

S (s) + O2 (g) → SO2 (g)
markschemeHtml:
«0.40%×500.0 g=» 2.0 «g» ✔

«2.0 g×1 mol S32.07 g=0.062 mol of S» = 0.062 «mol of SO2» ✔

 

Award [2] for correct final answer.

Accept 0.063 «mol».

meta.title: Calculate the amount, in mol, of sulfur dioxide produced when 500.0 g of lignite undergoes combustion. S (s) + O2 (g) → SO2 (g)
